mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate strengthening manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO month-over-month growth and forward 20-day SPX returns is not positive, or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it indicates severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8%, or average conditional return is not statistically negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2% and CPI inflation is above 2% YoY, real interest rates become deeply negative, forcing investors out of cash into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where the trigger condition holds, average 5-day SPX return is not positive or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-financial-crisis recovery regime, simultaneous improvement in housing starts and retail sales signals strengthening domestic demand and a self-sustaining recovery, which pushes equity prices higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: During the recovery regime, months with rising HOUST and RSAFS do not produce significantly positive 20-day SPX returns (two-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the signal rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When annual real GDP growth exceeds 2% as measured by the latest quarterly release, the economy is expanding robustly, which supports higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In quarters where GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 2%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return distribution is not significantly different from zero or the median return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payroll employment indicate a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and forward 20-day SPX returns is not positive over any consecutive 3-year period, or Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and nonfarm payrolls are rising simultaneously over a 3-month period, it signals broad-based economic strength and reduces recession risk, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both series show positive 3-month growth, the average 20-day SPX return is not positive, or the signal fails to rank outperform the median (Rank IC <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, it signals overheating that prompts future monetary tightening, increases discount rates, and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, the average 5-day SPX return is not negative or the win rate for negative returns is below 55%.
